Article Title: In a first, BCCI appoints three women's coaches at CoE
Article Text:
Nooshin Al Khadeer (ICC Photo)

MUMBAI: For probably the first time in its history, the Board of Control for Cricket in India has appointed a batch of three specialist women's coaches at its Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru. Former India cricketers Nooshin Al Khadeer (bowling coach), Sunetra Paranjpe (batting coach) and Vellaswamy Ramu Vanitha (fielding coach) have all been appointed as the batting, bowling and fielding coaches at CoE.Former India batting great VVS Laxman is the Head of Cricket at the BCCI ’s Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Bengaluru.Many former cricketers have been appointed as coaches at BCCI's CoE, but this is the first time that a batch of ex-India women's cricketers have been recruited as specialist coaches for women's cricket at the world-class facility, which was known as national cricket Academy earlier.Nooshin, who was an off-spinner, appeared in five Women's Tests (took 14 wickets@26.64), 78 WODIs (100 wickets@24.02) and two T20WIs (one wicket @41.00) for India from 2002 to 2012. She was the head coach of the victorious India Under-19 Women's teams in the 2023 and 2025 U-19 Women's T20 World Cups.Paranjpe, an ex-India allrounder, played three Women's Tests and 28 WODIs from 2002-07. She has been the head coach of the Mumbai women's team for the last three seasons (Mumbai clinched Women’s domestic T20 titles in black-to back seasons in the 2023-24 and 2024-25 during her tenure), and has been the head coach of Baroda for three seasons before that.Vanitha ‘VR’, a former batter, played six ODIs and 16 T20Is between 2014-16.Hailing the BCCI's decision to appoint three former India cricketers at CoE as coaches for the first time, former India women's team captain Diana Edulji told TOI, "It's a good step by the BCCI. It shows how much women's cricket has grown in India, and that the BCCI is taking it very seriously. It's another good development for Indian women's cricket after our team's maiden title triumph in the Women's World Cup last year. This trio will enable us to build the bench strength of women's cricket in the future, since these coaches are likely to go with India A, the Emerging women's team and India Under-19 tours."The BCCI has also hired three fast-bowling coaches-former India pacers Lakshmipathy Balaji and former first-class pacer P Krishna Kumar, at CoE. Former Karnataka batter Yere Goud has been hired as the batting coach, Former Rajasthan wicketkeeper Dishant Yagnik has taken over as a fielding coach at the CoE.Meanwhile, the Indian women’s cricket team is currently in England for an almost two-month long England tour, which also includes the historic one-off Women's Test at the iconic Lord’s cricket ground between India and England, which will be played from July 10-13. The 2026 Women's T20 World Cup will start on June 12, with England playing Sri Lanka at Birmingham on the opening night. India will launch their campaign against traditional arch-rivals Pakistan on June 14 at Birmingham. Placed in Group A, India will face Netherlands, South Africa, Bangladesh and Australia in the group stage.The India women's team will play a T20 warm-up match, Team India vs ECB Development XI at the Ambassador Cruise Line Ground in Chelmsford on Monday, and will train under lights at the same venue on Tuesday.JKCA's Rajesh Dhar is manager of Indian men's team for Afghanistan seriesIn possibly another first, Jammu & Kashmir Cricket Association's Rajesh Dhar has been appointed by the BCCI as the manager for the short home series, comprising a one-off Test at New Chandigarh, and three ODIs in June.
Article Summary: The Board of Control for Cricket in India has appointed a batch of three specialist women's coaches at its Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru. Former India cricketers Nooshin Al Khadeer, Sunetra Paranjpe and Vellaswamy Ramu Vanitha have all been appointed as the batting, bowling and fielding coaches. JKCA's Rajesh Dhar is manager of Indian men's team for Afghanistan series.

Article Title: Buffalo Sabres may pursue Patrick Kane after Alex Tuch contract uncertainty increases entering NHL offseason period
Article Text:
Patrick Kane #88 of the Detroit Red Wings skates against Alex Tuch #89 of the Buffalo Sabres during an NHL game on March 27, 2026 at KeyBank Center in Buffalo, New York. (Photo by Bill Wippert/NHLI via Getty Images)

Patrick Kane could strengthen Buffalo Sabres NHL offense after Alex Tuch departure



The Buffalo Sabres could make a big NHL offseason move if forward Alex Tuch doesn’t stay with the franchise long term. With the veteran winger’s contract in limbo, there has been speculation on potential replacements. Detroit Red Wings forward Patrick Kane has become a realistic option considering his scoring ability and connections to Western New York.The Sabres recently broke their long playoff drought and are trying to build a stronger postseason roster around their young core. If Alex Tuch's future with Buffalo is in question during NHL free agency discussions, adding a veteran like Patrick Kane could provide playoff experience, offensive depth and leadership. Alex Tuch’s exit could open the door for Patrick Kane to boost the Buffalo Sabres NHL offense. The veteran Detroit Red Wings forward may be in the twilight of his career, but he’s still one of the most experienced offensive forwards in the NHL. Patrick Kane posted 57 points in 67 games during the 2025-26 NHL season. Thus, providing Detroit with consistent playmaking and power play ability.Kane’s knack for setting up scoring chances could be key for Buffalo if Alex Tuch heads for the open market in free agency talks.Tuch has been a big contributor for the Sabres in recent seasons, but uncertainty over a possible contract extension has intensified NHL trade rumors and offseason speculation.Kane also brings a ton of postseason experience. The former Chicago Blackhawks star won three Stanley Cups and was a steady playoff performer during his NHL career. Another big piece is Kane’s Buffalo ties. Tuch isn't the only one with Buffalo ties, as the native grew up a Sabres fan, making the prospect of a move to his hometown even more enticing.The Sabres would still have to be mindful of salary cap flexibility from a financial perspective. Patrick Kane’s recent contracts have included a lot of bonuses and short-term structures, so Buffalo could theoretically go for a one-year deal and avoid a long-term commitment.If Alex Tuch stays in Buffalo, the Sabres franchise will look to maintain its current core. But if things get rough, Patrick Kane could be one of the more interesting options available in the NHL offseason to replace.
Article Summary: The Buffalo Sabres could make a big NHL offseason move if forward Alex Tuch doesn’t stay with the franchise long term. Detroit Red Wings forward Patrick Kane has become a realistic option considering his scoring ability and connections to Western New York. Tuch isn't the only one with Buffalo ties, as the native grew up a Sabres fan.

Article Title: 10 pink lakes around the world: From Lake Hillier to Pink Lake
Article Text:
pc: wikipedia

Pink lakes in the world that look almost unreal



1.

Lake Hillier

, Australia

pc: wikipedia

2. Hutt Lagoon, Australia

pc: wikipedia

3. Las Coloradas, Mexico

pc: wikipedia

4. Retba Lake, Senegal

pc: wikipedia

5.

Laguna Colorada

, Bolivia

pc: wikipedia

6. Dusty Rose Lake, Canada

pc: wikipedia

7.

Masazirgol Lake

, Azerbaijan

8. Lake MacDonnell, Australia

9. Salina de Torrevieja, Spain

10.

Pink Lake

, Australia

pc: wikipedia







Across several continents, some lakes appear almost unreal at first glance. Some turn pale rose during dry months, others deepen into bubblegum pink under harsh sunlight, and a few shift colour throughout the year depending on salt concentration and microscopic algae activity. The effect is not caused by pollution or minerals alone, as many people assume, but by a combination of salinity, bacteria, temperature and seasonal evaporation. A number of these lakes sit in remote deserts or coastal flats where water movement is limited, allowing unusual ecosystems to develop over time. Others exist close to towns and tourist routes, though their colour still feels oddly disconnected from the surrounding landscape. Together, they form one of the stranger natural patterns found across the planet.According to WorldAtlas , check the list of the 10 pink lakes around the world.On Middle Island off the southern coast of Western Australia, Lake Hillier remains one of the best-known pink lakes in the world. The lake sits beside the ocean, separated only by a narrow strip of sand and vegetation, creating a sharp contrast between dark blue seawater and dense pink water inland.Scientists have linked the colour largely to salt-loving microorganisms and algae that thrive in extremely saline conditions. What makes Hillier unusual is the stability of its colour. Even when collected in containers, the water often keeps its pink tone rather than fading immediately after removal.Hutt Lagoon lies near the coastal settlement of Port Gregory in Western Australia and changes appearance regularly throughout the year. At certain times, it turns soft lilac or salmon pink, while dry and sunny periods can produce far stronger shades.The lake is commercially important as well. Algae harvested from its waters are used in the production of beta carotene, giving the site an industrial role alongside its growing tourism appeal. Roads nearby allow visitors to see the changing colours without travelling far into remote regions.On the northern edge of the Yucatán Peninsula, Las Coloradas looks less like a natural lake system and more like a series of painted pools. The area is tied closely to salt production, and many of the bright pink sections are part of evaporation ponds rather than untouched lakes.High salt concentration encourages the growth of microorganisms that release reddish pigments into the water. Under strong daylight, the colour can appear almost artificial, though it fades and intensifies depending on the weather and water depth.Known locally as Lac Rose, Lake Retba sits northeast of Dakar and was once widely recognised for its striking rose tint. The lake became famous partly because of the large-scale salt harvesting carried out by workers who spend long hours in highly saline water.Its colour varies more than it once did, especially after periods of flooding and changing rainfall patterns. During hotter and drier stretches, the pink tones tend to return more strongly as evaporation raises salt concentration again.Laguna Colorada in Bolivia sits high in the Andes within a windswept volcanic landscape. The water here often shifts between rusty red, orange and pink rather than maintaining a single tone throughout the year.Mineral deposits, algae and changing light conditions all contribute to the colour variation. Flamingos gather in large numbers around the shallow lake, feeding on microorganisms that survive in the harsh high-altitude environment.British Columbia’s Dusty Rose Lake is less widely known than Australia’s pink lakes, partly because of its remote location near rugged mountain terrain. The water carries a muted rose colour rather than the bright pink shades seen in highly saline lakes.Researchers believe glacial runoff and fine mineral particles play a role in the colouring. Access remains difficult, which has limited large-scale tourism around the lake.Near Baku, Masazirgol Lake has long been associated with salt extraction. In warmer months, parts of the lake develop pink and reddish hues caused by algae and salt-loving microorganisms concentrated in shallow sections.Industrial activity exists around the lake, though the unusual colouring continues to attract photographers and visitors. The surrounding landscape is comparatively dry and flat, making the coloured water stand out sharply from a distance.Lake MacDonnell on South Australia’s Eyre Peninsula is known for a narrow road that cuts between bright pink water on one side and turquoise water on the other. The contrast has made the site especially popular in aerial photography.Salt production operations have shaped much of the surrounding area. Different salinity levels across connected ponds create variations in colour that shift gradually along the lake system.In southeastern Spain, the salt lake at Torrevieja forms part of a protected wetland region that also supports flamingo populations and migratory birds. The water can appear pink during periods of strong algae growth combined with high salinity.The lake has historically supported large salt extraction industries, though it has also become a major attraction for visitors travelling through the Alicante region. During calm weather, the pink surface often reflects surrounding clouds and white salt banks.Australia has several lakes literally named Pink Lake, though the one near Esperance became particularly famous decades ago for its strong colouring. Over time, environmental changes and altered salinity levels reduced the intensity of the pink appearance in some sections.Even so, the lake remains closely associated with the country’s unusual salt landscapes. Seasonal conditions still influence its colour, and nearby regions continue to contain active pink lakes shaped by similar environmental processes.
Article Summary: Pink lakes vary in colour depending on salt concentration and microscopic algae activity. The effect is not caused by pollution or minerals alone, but by a combination of salinity, bacteria, temperature and seasonal evaporation. A number of these lakes sit in remote deserts or coastal flats where water movement is limited, allowing unusual ecosystems to develop.

Article Title: Samardeep Gill beats national record holder Tajinderpal Singh Toor to clinch gold at Federation Cup
Article Text:
Samardeep Gill (Image credit: X)

Results:

NEW DELHI: Madhya Pradesh’s Samardeep Singh Gill outshone seasoned Punjab shot putter Tajinderpal Singh Toor to clinch the gold medal in the men’s shot put event on the final day of the National Senior Federation Competition on Monday.Two-time Asian Games champion Toor opened strongly with a throw of 20.07m, but the 25-year-old Gill stole the lead in his fourth attempt with a massive 20.46m effort, which eventually secured him the gold medal.Toor, who holds the national record of 21.77m, tried hard to bounce back but could only manage 20.06m in his final attempt.Gill also surpassed the Athletics Federation of India’s (AFI) qualification mark of 20.36m for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.In the men’s 400m hurdles, Karnataka’s Yashas P and NCOE Trivandrum’s Santhosh Kumar T both crossed the CWG qualification mark. Yashas claimed gold with a timing of 49.00 seconds, while Santhosh Kumar finished a close second in 49.06 seconds.Meanwhile, Maharashtra’s promising javelin thrower Shivam Lohakare bagged the gold medal in the men’s event with a best throw of 81.71m. The qualification mark for the Commonwealth Games in men’s javelin stands at 82.61m.Men: 200m: Animesh Kujur (Odisha) 20.64 secs, Jishu Prasad (Kerala) 20.98 secs, Abhay Singh (Reliance) 21.01 secs.5,000m: Shivaji Parashuram (Karnataka) 14:14.43 secs, Vinod Singh (Madhya Pradesh) 14:15.50 secs, Deepak Bhatt (Uttarakhand) 14:17.72 secs.400m hurdles: Yashas P (Karnataka) 49.00 secs, Santhosh Kumar T (NCOE Trivandrum) 49.06 secs, Subhas Das (Navy) 50.51 secs.Javelin throw: Shivam Lohakare (Maharashtra) 81.71m, Yashvir Singh (Rajasthan) 80.80m, Rohit Yadav (Railway Sports) 80.40m.Shot put: Samardeep Singh Gill (Madhya Pradesh) 20.46m, Tajinderpal Singh Toor (Punjab) 20.07m, Karanveer Singh (Punjab) 19.38m.Women: Kusum Thakur (Himachal Pradesh) 23.94 secs, Tamana (Haryana) 24.06 secs, Sudeshna H (Maharashtra) 24.07 secs.5,000m: Seema (Reliance) 16:04.83, Ravina Gayakwad (Maharashtra) 16:46.21, Sonam Parmar (IOCL) 16:48.11.400m hurdles: Vithya Ramraj (Tamil Nadu) 56.61 secs, Sinchal Kaveramma T (NCOE Bengaluru) 57.77 secs, Shravani Sachin (Maharashtra) 59.73 secs.High jump: Khyati Mathur (Uttar Pradesh) 1.82m, Manshi (Railway Sports) 1.80m, Abhinaya S Shetty (JSW) 1.73m.Heptathlon: Anamika KA (Kerala) 5685 points, Pooja (JSW) 5632 points, Khushi (Haryana) 5435 points.
Article Summary: Madhya Pradesh’s Samardeep Singh Gill outshone seasoned Punjab shot putter Tajinderpal Singh Toor to clinch the gold medal. Gill also surpassed the Athletics Federation of India's (AFI) qualification mark of 20.36m for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games. In the men's 400m hurdles, Yashas P and NCOE Trivandrum's Santhosh Kumar T both crossed the CWG qualification mark.

Article Title: Poor home record to toothless bowling and slow batting: Inside Delhi Capitals’ failed IPL 2026 campaign
Article Text:
Photo/BCCI

Home disadvantage



Not capitalising on moments and bad catching



Lack of wickets



Absence of Starc in most games



Keeping it slow



'Open chested' Kuldeep



IPL 2026: DC report card



What next for DC?



Despite ending their IPL 2026 campaign with a 40-run win over Kolkata Knight Riders in Kolkata on Sunday, Delhi Capitals once again finished outside the playoffs.DC ended sixth in the table with seven wins and seven defeats, extending their playoff drought to five straight seasons. It was also the second consecutive season in which the combination of head coach Hemang Badani and captain Axar Patel failed to guide the franchise into the top four.Delhi showed flashes of being a strong side during the season and at some stages looked capable of making the playoffs. But poor performances at home, lack of wickets, poor catching, among others combined to derail their campaign once again.While Delhi struggled for consistency through the season, their poor performances at home once again played a major role in their disappointing campaign.DC played seven matches at the Arun Jaitley Stadium and won only two of them . Their first home victory came against Mumbai Indians on April 4 by six wickets, while the second arrived much later against Rajasthan Royals last week by five wickets. Between those wins, they lost five consecutive home matches against Gujarat Titans, Punjab Kings, Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Chennai Super Kings and KKR.The Capitals struggled to understand the varying nature of the Kotla pitch and often failed to settle on the right strategy or playing combination for home games.There were several games where DC came close to victory but failed to finish the job. They lost to Gujarat Titans by one run at home and were also unable to defend a total of 264 against Punjab Kings.Dropped catches added to their problems and proved costly in defeats against Chennai Super Kings and Sunrisers Hyderabad.Looking back at the season, where DC finished sixth despite starting with two wins, Badani said things could have turned out differently had the side grabbed key moments during matches."There were many games where I genuinely felt that the game could go either way and we didn't seize those moments," Badani said in the press conference after win over Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR). "One being the Gujarat [Titans] game, we lost the game there by one run. The [failure to defend] 264 [against Punjab Kings] is again something that you would try and seize or try to defend that score. With CSK [Chennai Super Kings], we dropped some catches, and with SRH [Sunrisers Hyderabad], we dropped some catches in the crucial time of the game. "Apart from losing crucial matches, Delhi’s inability to take wickets throughout the season also hurt their campaign badly. Even after bowling out KKR in their final match, DC ended IPL 2026 with only 64 wickets in 14 matches, joint-lowest among all teams.DC bowlers sent down 254.3 overs during the season and averaged 39.92, which was the second worst among all sides, ahead of only Punjab Kings (42.54). Their bowling strike rate of 23.8 was also better than only Punjab Kings (24.5).Mitchell Starc playing only six matches was another bad news for Delhi. The left-arm pacer picked up 11 wickets at an average of 20.36 and still finished as DC’s joint second-highest wicket-taker alongside Axar Patel. Only Lungi Ngidi , with 13 wickets in 11 games, took more wickets for DC.Badani admitted Starc’s absence affected the team, but also pointed out that DC still had opportunities to qualify."Starc is obviously somebody who's done exceedingly well in all formats and him not being available to us the first nine games does hamper our progress. But I think, on the whole, even without him, we had our moments. Even without him, we had our chances to qualify. Starc does make a huge difference. But I think, as a side, I genuinely feel that if we had played those small margins… it's a game of small margins. Literally, very small margins. "Milestones like fifties and centuries do not matter much in T20 cricket, which makes batting strike rate a far more important metric. In terms of overall strike rate during the league stage, DC ranked fourth from the bottom, ahead of only Chennai Super Kings (147.71), Lucknow Super Giants (145.03), and Kolkata Knight Riders (147.16).Delhi Capitals batters scored at an overall strike rate of 149.29 in IPL 2026.A closer look at the numbers highlight the issue even further. Among the seven DC batters who faced at least 100 deliveries during the season, only one batter had a strike rate above 170 — KL Rahul Rahul scored 593 runs in 14 innings at a strike rate of 174.41.Kuldeep Yadav was named Player of the Match for figures of 3/29 against KKR in Delhi’s final league match, but the left-arm wrist spinner admitted he did not have a good IPL season overall.Before the KKR match, Kuldeep had managed only seven wickets in ten innings at an average of 50.28 and an economy rate of 10.66. After DC’s defeat to Chennai Super Kings two weeks earlier, Axar Patel had even remarked that "when we were bowling, I missed my partner Kuldeep".Speaking after the KKR game, Kuldeep explained the technical issue he felt affected his bowling during the season."I thought I was bowling a bit more open chest, and sometimes you're not using your full body. I just pushed the ball, and that's where the T20 batters are extremely strong to hit deep down the ground and play back foot. Once you start using more of your body and try to spin the ball harder, and ultimately you see the result, you get the dip and the drift, and obviously you can vary the pace as well. So it was lovely to see. I think it was a lovely game for me. "Despite the disappointing campaign, a few players gave DC genuine reasons for optimism. KL Rahul became the batting pillar of the side. He not only scored 593 runs in 14 games, but also improved his strike rate, hitting at 174.41 in the season and smashed one century and five half-centuries, often carrying the batting lineup during difficult periods.Youngsters Sameer Rizvi and Ashutosh Sharma also impressed. Ashutosh, in particular, stood out with fearless strokeplay and a strike rate touching 181.91 in limited opportunities.With the ball, Lungi Ngidi emerged as DC's most reliable wicket-taking pacer, claiming 13 wickets in a difficult season for bowlers. In the three games that he played, young pacer Madhav Tiwari also showed promise and took four wickets at an economy of 8.70.The next IPL season will bring another shift in control for Delhi Capitals, with co-ownership moving back from GMR Sports to JSW Sports for the next two years.Will Axar Patel continue to lead the side in IPL 2027, or DC need a complete reset, once again? Perhaps it's too early to answer those questions as nobody knows what's the thinking inside the management.However, what's known with certainty is that the IPL 2026 became another season full of missed opportunities for DC and they remain alongside Punjab Kings among the two teams who are part of the league since its beginning in 2008 but are yet to lift the IPL trophy.
Article Summary: Delhi Capitals ended their IPL 2026 campaign with a 40-run win over Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) DC ended sixth in the table with seven wins and seven defeats, extending their playoff drought to five straight seasons. Head coach Hemang Badani said things could have turned out differently had the side grabbed key moments during matches.

Article Title: BTS to release a 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem? Viral AMA theory sends ARMY into meltdown
Article Text:
A viral fan theory suggesting BTS could announce a 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem or an Arirang deluxe edition at the AMAs has sent ARMY into frenzy online. The speculation gained momentum after reports linked BTS to FIFA’s halftime show plans and HYBE’s stock surge, with fans flooding social media with shocked reactions, theories, and excitement.

BTS FIFA World Cup rumors fuel massive fan excitement online

HYBE’s rising momentum adds more weight to BTS speculation

This week, one fan theory was enough to send BTS fans into full detective mode. One short prediction, the group may reveal either an Arirang deluxe edition or FIFA World Cup single at the AMAs, quickly went viral on social media and online fan communities, igniting a global chatter overnight.The reaction image attached to the post became part of the frenzy. Fans flooded timelines with shocked memes, countdown theories and emotional reactions as speculation around BTS and the 2026 FIFA World Cup continued to grow. The group gave no official word, but the buzz alone was a testament to how quickly BTS can take over online culture with just one rumor.The now viral post suggested BTS may be preparing a major announcement tied to the FIFA World Cup 2026 era. That theory gained even more traction because the group has already been linked heavily to the tournament’s halftime show discussions in recent weeks.Many ARMY members pointed toward the timing of BTS’ recent activities, including the success of Arirang, as a possible clue that another global anthem could arrive soon. Others believe HYBE may use a high-profile award show moment to reveal a deluxe album expansion, especially after the original project shattered streaming and sales records earlier this year.What made the rumor explode was not only the theory itself, but the emotional fan reaction attached to it. The meme image showing a stunned reaction mirrored exactly how fans felt. Within hours, reposts and quote posts pushed the discussion across multiple platforms, with users debating everything from possible collaborators to whether the rumored FIFA song could follow the global impact of “Dreamers” and previous World Cup tracks.The timing of the rumor also matters because BTS are currently driving enormous momentum for HYBE. Recent reports revealed the company experienced a major stock surge after FIFA-related BTS announcements sparked investor confidence.According to market reports, HYBE shares climbed sharply following news connected to the group’s potential involvement in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final halftime show. Analysts also credited BTS’ blockbuster Arirang era for boosting revenue growth and restoring confidence in HYBE’s global expansion plans.One industry analyst stated, “HYBE’s strong earnings performance is expected to continue from the second quarter. With BTS’s year-end tour projected to attract over 4 million attendees and other intellectual properties (IPs) under HYBE set to ramp up activities, the outlook remains positive.”For fans, though, the financial success is only part of the story. Right now, the real excitement comes from the possibility that BTS may once again deliver a worldwide cultural moment that stretches far beyond music.
Article Summary: A viral fan theory suggests BTS could announce a 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem or an Arirang deluxe edition at the AMAs. The speculation gained momentum after reports linked BTS to FIFA’s halftime show plans. The group gave no official word, but the buzz alone was a testament to how quickly BTS can take over online culture.
